content.body:
Any chance we could run these snippets with some internal test runner and log the boundaries? e.g. d8 (the v8 shell) has `--print-scopes`.

The reason is that for the spec text I'd allow the maxium boundary possible but it would be nice if we could recommend where generators put range start/end if all engines agree somewhat.
content.formatted_body:
Any chance we could run these snippets with some internal test runner and log the boundaries? e.g. d8 (the v8 shell) has --print-scopes.

The reason is that for the spec text I'd allow the maxium boundary possible but it would be nice if we could recommend where generators put range start/end if all engines agree somewhat.

content.body: I have a draft up how we could give generators some boundaries where to put start/end for generated ranges for JS: https://tc39.es/ecma426/pr/249/#sec-generated-range-record-js. Previously I did this via a record and SDO over ECMA-262 productions but I think a simple table like the PR does should be sufficient. We can discuss in the next scopes meeting :)
